justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Pre-commit offset pattern identified as fatal flaw causing silent data loss on crash — called 'stop-ship' and explained the exact window where events exist in zero durable systems. (2) Explicitly explains that retry logic and DLQ do not protect against pre-commit crash — 'retries only trigger on processing failures, not on crashes before processing starts' and 'An event lost between commit and processing never enters the retry path, so it never reaches the DLQ.' (3) Verdict is NOT READY. (4) Specific remediation: commit after processing + DLQ publish, with correct ordering diagram. (5) Correctly notes observability signals are otherwise adequate — 'logging, health endpoint, Prometheus metrics, retry logic, DLQ, and circuit breaker are all the right instincts' while not treating them as compensating for the flaw.

observations.text: Exceptionally clear walkthrough of the failure mode with numbered steps showing exactly where the crash window exists
Correctly identifies that DLQ and retry logic are defeated by the pre-commit pattern — 'turns every other resilience mechanism into a lie'
Provides the correct fix with concrete code ordering and notes the need for idempotent processing as a consequence
Identifies additional failure modes beyond the primary flaw: DLQ unavailability, poison messages, rebalance during processing, missing processing timeout
Correctly notes rollback plan inherits the same gap — events consumed between deploy and rollback are also lost
Well-structured prioritization: stop-ship vs should-have vs nice-to-have, with clear rationale for each
